page: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and a substantial 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional memory and database performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth, recording 406.47 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 183.77 GB/sec for large block write operations. Passmark database operations score in the top 10% at 47278.09, and Redis SET operations reach 67.73 million operations per second. Multi-core capabilities are also strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21629.0 and a Passmark memory score of 3409.94.

While the arm64 Neoverse-V2 architecture delivers high resource density and excellent memory throughput, certain workloads reveal performance limitations. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck; prompt processing for a 70B model falls into the bottom 25% at 5.91 tokens/sec. However, for non-GPU workloads, the server offers high qualitative cost efficiency by combining massive local NVMe storage with high-speed memory bandwidth on an energy-efficient ARM architecture.

This server is designed for storage-intensive and database-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for relational and non-relational databases, caching layers like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and data compilation tasks. It is less suited for heavy AI/ML training or large-scale LLM inference due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware and lower performance on large model prompts.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of system memory and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor with a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for memory bandwidth, with large block read-only speeds of 406.47 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring 47278.09 in Passmark. It also demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and multi-core processing. However, it exhibits weaker performance in large-scale LLM inference, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 25%. This server is highly efficient for high-throughput databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing, offering a balanced, storage-heavy profile without dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 7500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.8xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server featuring 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 7500 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized platform delivering massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read/write bandwidth
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Excellent fit for high-throughput databases and Redis caching layers
Limited performance for large-scale LLM inference workloads
